raw_text: tomás costa costto@outlook.com +351 913 420 924 www.linkedin.com/in/tomas-costa3 personal profile highly motivated third-year mechanical engineering student at northumbria university, with hands-on experience in fuel systems engineering, product development, and engineering research. strong problem-solving, teamwork, and organisational skills, with a proven track record of supporting high-profile aircraft projects at airbus uk. proficient in cad, cfd, and matlab/simulink, with a keen interest in innovative engineering solutions. fluent in portuguese, english, and german. aspiring to pursue a master's in aircraft systems integration as part of uc3m-airbus 2025-2026 masi programme. education northumbria university newcastle newcastle upon tyne, uk beng (hons) mechanical engineering september 2021 present expected mark: first-class honours current average mark: 75% relevant module marks: thermodynamics (84%), statics and dynamics (94%), fluids and energy (76%) activities: course representative currently working on a final-year research project, consisting of developing a cfd model capable of accurately determining the auto-ignition process of aviation fuels within aircraft fuel tanks. deutsche schule zu porto porto, portugal high school september 2008 july 2020 diploma: zeugnis der allgemeinen hochschulreife work experience airbus uk filton, bristol, uk fuel systems engineering intern august 2023 august 2024 1 year-long engineering placement in the fuel & inerting systems department: e requirements management refined technical writing skills and attention to detail by authoring and editing requirement documents (e.g., equipment specifications) using rational doors, ensuring improved organisation of the requirement databases of the a321 and a350 aircraft programmes. employed problem-solving skills when devising a strategy to deliver updated a350 fuel system requirements documentation ahead of type certification, ensuring timely approval. e product development strengthened analytical, data analysis, and cfd skills by performing fuel system technical analysis (e.g., water/fuel scavenge-, unusable/usable fuel-, and transfer analysis), optimising the fuel system designs of the following aircraft: a350-1000 ulr, a321 xlr, and a future aircraft concept. e continuous product development (cpd) improved teamwork and supplier management skills by actively collaborating with the supplier of fluid mechanical equipment to resolve in-service problems of the a350 xwb aircraft programme. developed expertise in assessing requirement deviations and identifying substitutes for obsolete parts. e knowledge management demonstrated leadership and organisational skills by spearheading a knowledge transfer initiative, authoring and uploading 31 technical reports to the fuel systems domain database, improving access to critical knowledge. northumbria university - thermal energy storage applications newcastle upon tyne, uk research intern september 2022 july 2023 e refined cad and manufacturing skills by designing and machining moulds for thermal material samples. e improved data analysis skills by testing the samples for thermophysical properties, contributing to the successful validation of new potential energy storage materials. e advanced sustainable energy storage methods by co-authoring the following research papers: 1. thermophysical properties of tetrabutylammonium chloride, paraffin and fatty acids for thermal energy applications (link) 2. experimental investigation on the thermal performance of low-temperature om55/gnps/span80 nanocomposites (link) northumbria engineering society newcastle upon tyne, uk social secretary september 2022 july 2023 e honed planning and organisation skills by organising guest speaker events, industry trips, and social activities for members, fostering engagement and collaboration among over 150 engineering students. enactus northumbria newcastle upon tyne,
